title: Jeng Droga
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jeng}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Jeng_EE.jpg]] |name=Jeng Droga |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin IV]][[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair=Blond |feathers= |eyes=Red[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] *[[Dark Empire]] *[[Second Imperium]] |masters=[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My lord, you are not well…|First mate of the ''Emperor's Shadow''|{{JournalCite|7|The Kaal Connection}}}} '''Jeng Droga''' was an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] who served [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]] practitioner, he was fanatically loyal and dedicated to his master, with whom he shared a psychic link. Droga, who captained the [[Emperor's Shadow (shuttle)|''Emperor's Shadow'']], oversaw the other Emperor's Hands and accompanied Palpatine on any hazardous missions. On several occasions, he saved Palpatine's life—first, when Palpatine was attacked by the specters of long-dead [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], and secondly on [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]], when the Emperor's spirit journeyed from [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and bonded with Droga. The Emperor's Hand carried Palpatine's essence inside him all the way to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], where [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] extracted the Emperor's spirit from Droga. When Palpatine struck at the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], his [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies suffered sabotage and destruction from various sources. Inhabiting his final clone body, the Emperor soon learned that the body's cell structure was deteriorating due to further sabotage, and that it would soon expire. Faced with the prospect of having no body to possess, Palpatine planned to inhabit the infant [[Anakin Solo]], the grandson of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. Droga strongly cautioned against the plan, and he was proven right when Palpatine died his final [[Death/Legends|death]] at the hands of [[Empatojayos Brand]]. Driven to insanity by the loss of his master, Droga's hope of a returned Palpatine was rekindled by the [[Second Imperium]], which appeared to be headed by the Emperor. He soon realized that the Second Imperium's Palpatine was little more than a scam, and he found himself left with no cause to fight for. Droga was eventually struck down by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyle Katarn]]. ==Biography== ===Emperor's Hand=== [[File:JengDroga.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Jeng Droga, with his dual lightsabers drawn]] The most loyal of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] servants, Jeng Droga was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. He had lived an erratic life, and had poured his energies and emotions into mastering the art of the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. In particular, he was a practitioner of the [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]] form, which allowed him to wield his two lightsabers, one red-hued and one yellow-hued, in each hand.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}} He and Palpatine were bonded through a psychic link—a link that the Emperor would frequently use to his advantage. Droga was tasked with supervising his fellow Hands and with guarding Palpatine's personal yacht, the [[Emperor's Shadow (shuttle)|''Emperor's Shadow'']], of which he was also captain. When Palpatine had [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] of himself created, he had Droga accompany him to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], where the duplicate bodies were developed. The Emperor's Hand also traveled with his master to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] world of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], home to the tombs of long-dead [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Palpatine demanded knowledge of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] from the entombed and mummified Sith Lords, sparking their rage. They cowed the Emperor, prompting Droga to rush to Palpatine's rescue. He returned his master to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], before journeying to Byss to retrieve one of Palpatine's clones. ===Savior=== {{Quote|But Excellency, that would be too ''dangerous''. You must stay aboard ''Eclipse II''. ''We'' will go down and capture the Jedi child.''"
"''Dangerous…? In the entire galaxy, there is ''nothing'' that I fear. It is ''they'' who fear ''me''.|Droga and Palpatine|}} In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Droga was sent to the [[Yushan sector/Legends|Yushan sector]], where he was to await his master's instructions, while Palpatine himself oversaw construction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. During the pivotal [[Battle of Endor/Legends|battle]] that took place there, the Emperor [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|died]] his first [[Death/Legends|death]] at the hands of his apprentice, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Vader]].{{Film|VI}} When Droga reached the [[Yushan sector/Legends|Yushan sector]], he felt his master's death through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. At that moment, aboard the ''Emperor's Shadow'', Droga's body wrenched in pain, and he began screaming. He then fell into a state of insanity, crushing the windpipe of one of his crew members before taking his dual lightsabers to the rest of his subordinates. In his rampage, he smashed the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] controls of the ''Emperor's Shadow'', causing the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to plummet into one of the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]]. Palpatine's spirit, which had survived Endor, journeyed all the way to Kaal and entered Droga's body. Now infused with Palpatine's tremendous dark energy, Droga was driven to madness. In a process said to have taken years, [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] extracted Palpatine's essence from Droga's body, allowing the Emperor to inhabit one of his clone bodies. For the second time, the Emperor's Hand had saved his master's life. [[File:DrogaEclipse.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Droga and the captain of the ''Eclipse II'' meet with Palpatine.]] While others, such as Pestage, [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Ysanne Isard]], [[Trioculus]], and [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], attempted to unify the Empire in the wake of Palpatine's first death, the Emperor himself lay in wait on Byss. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], he chose to strike, sending his united Imperial forces against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Over the next year, he would battle [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his sister [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], and would perish multiple times.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Following the destruction of the [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|dreadnought]] [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']], the remainder of Palpatine's spare clone bodies were destroyed by [[Nefta]] and [[Sa-Di]], Dark Side Adepts who were planning to take Palpatine's place as ruler of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] The Emperor had transferred his consciousness to one last clone body, but that body had been subject to sabotage in the form of genetic damage. In need of a new body, as his current one was rapidly deteriorating, Palpatine planned to transfer his essence to the infant [[Anakin Solo]], a direct descendant of Anakin Skywalker, the [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]] of Jedi lore. In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Droga accompanied him aboard the Star Dreadnought ''[[Eclipse II]]'' and cautioned against his master's trip to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], where Solo was in hiding. Droga suggested that he and the rest of Palpatine's servants journey to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to retrieve the child, but the Emperor paid Droga no heed. Still, a vision of his master in peril haunted Droga, and he rushed to Onderon to again come to the aid of Palpatine. He found that he was too late, and that his master had died his final death—the Emperor's spirit had been bound to that of the dying [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Empatojayos Brand]] and subsequently dragged into the [[Netherworld of the Force/Legends|Netherworld of the Force]]. Droga let out a mental scream that echoed through the Force for days. Feeling personally responsible for the demise of Palpatine, Droga journeyed to Kaal, where Palpatine's spirit had possessed him. There, he waited for his master to return once more. ===Berserker=== In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]] Droga placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on the head of fellow Emperor's Hand [[Sarcev Quest]], who had been involved in the sabotage of Palpatine's extra clone bodies. At the same time, he dispatched [[darksider]]s to find and capture Quest. The 100,000-[[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] bounty went to [[Ailyn Vel]], who was disguised as her father, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. Droga subsequently executed Quest.{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} That same year, twelve years after what was apparently Palpatine's last demise, the Emperor seemed to return from the dead yet again to head the [[Second Imperium]]. With the intent of joining his master once more, Droga participated in the [[Third Battle of Yavin]], where he fought against [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[New Jedi Order]]. During the battle, however, he realized that his master had not returned and that the Palpatine of the Second Imperium was but a scam. With nothing left to lose, Droga entered into a berserk fury, furiously battling the Jedi. Subsequently, Droga was struck down by [[Battlemaster/Legends|Jedi Battlemaster]] [[Kyle Katarn]].It was the intent of ''The Emperor's Pawns'' and ''Kyle Katarn's Tale'' author Abel G. Peña to have Droga perish at Yavin, thus giving a canonical conclusion to the "Droga's Demise" adventure seed within ''The Emperor's Pawns''. (see: {{JCFCite|url=posts/19193666|board=Literature|thread=Kyle Katarn's Tale (Dark Forces Articles by Abel & Jason)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2005-04-12|time=6:09 PM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/obNTS}} In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], he would be written about in passing by [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Let me go to him!|Jeng Droga|}} Droga was fanatical, fiercely dedicated and loyal to his Emperor. He was genuinely devoted to Palpatine, as an apprentice would their master, and was generally a somber being. Droga was introspective as well, but his experiences amplified his emotions. He kept himself in solitude and dedicated himself to mastering the art of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. When harm befell Palpatine, Droga would generally react violently, falling into utter insanity. When he had nothing left to lose, he became a berserker and a dangerous adversary in combat. Droga felt that it was his personal responsibility to ensure his master's safety and survival, and was devastated when Palpatine finally perished on Onderon. ==Powers and abilities== Jeng Droga was adept at a number of [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], including [[battlemind]], [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]], [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]], [[Mind trick/Legends|affect mind]], and [[Force Enrage|Force rage]]. ==Behind the scenes== Jeng Droga first appeared in the introduction of the article ''[[The Kaal Connection]]'', written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] and [[Timothy Zahn]], and published in [[1995]]'s [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 7|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 7]]. The article ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'', published in [[2001]]'s [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]] and written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Juan Schwartz]], greatly expanded upon the character and featured artwork of Droga by [[Joe Corroney]]. Corroney's depiction was in turn based on a previously unidentified character from [[Tom Veitch]]'s [[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]], which had originally been published shortly after the ''Star Wars Adventure Journal''. The image, as well as the accompanying text, retroactively consolidated the unidentified character from ''Empire's End'' and Droga.{{JCFCite|url=posts/3425564|board=Literature|thread=The Emperor's Pawns article (was: Gamer #5 stuff @ TOS!!!)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2001-07-16|time=9:22 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200420171538/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-emperors-pawns-article-was-gamer-5-stuff-tos.3223225/page-4}} The character was briefly used by Peña again in ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'' and [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], and was mentioned in [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]. ''[[The Kaal Connection]]'' RPG adventures offers an optional adventure hook where the player characters look for (and find) Droga's remains in Kaal. As Droga's survival of his emergency landing in Kaal became canon in later sources, this hook fell out of [[Star Wars Legends|continuity]]. Droga is absent from the [[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]] and his role is instead given to the Dark Side Adept [[T'iaz]]. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|7|The Kaal Connection|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] {{Ret}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jeng Droga}} *{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}} *{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pl=Jeng Droga }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Droga, Jeng}} [[Category:Emperor's Hands]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Personnel of the Dark Empire]] [[Category:Second Imperium individuals]]
